track.title: Agent Engineering
track.description: Minimal catalog for agent systems — tools, MCP, planning, and failure modes.

nodes.title: Tool use patterns
outcomes: Define a tool schema the model can call
Handle tool results and continue the loop
Bound arguments and validate tool outputs
rubric: Writes a clear tool JSON schema
Names a validation rule for tool args

nodes.title: MCP and connectors
outcomes: Explain MCP client/server roles
Expose a simple MCP tool or resource
Connect an agent to an MCP server
rubric: Shows how an agent discovers MCP tools

nodes.title: Planning loops
outcomes: Implement a plan-act-observe loop
Break a goal into tool-sized steps
Stop when success criteria are met

nodes.title: Agent memory
outcomes: Distinguish working memory vs durable store
Persist useful facts across sessions
Avoid stuffing irrelevant history into context

nodes.title: Failure modes
outcomes: Detect and break infinite tool loops
Add guardrails for destructive tools
Recover gracefully from tool errors
rubric: Names at least two agent failure modes
Describes a safe failure for tool errors

nodes.title: Agent observability
outcomes: Trace each tool call and model turn
Log decisions that led to a bad outcome
Score agent runs with simple evals
rubric: Links a failure to a logged span
Names a basic agent eval signal

nodes.title: Ship an agent
outcomes: Ship a single-purpose agent with tool allowlist
Document runbook for operators
Set cost and latency budgets
rubric: Defines allowlisted tools for a use case
